senses.gloss: someone who covers, coats things; gilder, silverer
swindler, cheat, imposter, charlatan, mountebank, liar

etymology.text: Belongs to د ج ل (d j l) now, with a verb دَجَلَ (dajala, “dupe, cheat, swindle, deceive”), but the early religious use suggests that these meanings are a semantic loan from Classical Syriac ܕܰܓܳܠܳܐ (daggālā, “liar”), also used for the False Messiah, whereas the Arabic root prominently means “smearing things over other things”, “coating”.
